cot_prefix: Okay, let's find the correct answer. First, I need to go through the passage and understand what each part is saying. The passage is about the improvements in the water supply in southern Beijing, particularly focusing on recycled water plants and sewage treatment. It also mentions the transition from coal-burning stoves to electric radiators as part of a three-year plan. Let's break down the key points from the passage: 1. The quality of water supply in southern Beijing has been improving. 2. The government is upgrading three recycled water plants, increasing the processing capacity by 160,000 cubic meters per day. 3. More sewage treatment plants are being built to recycle sewage. 4. Recycled water use should be promoted, especially in water-scarce cities like Beijing. This is because recycling water is cheaper than transferring it over long distances. 5. Beijing plans to set up 46 more recycled water plants and upgrade 20 sewage treatment plants in the next three years. 6. The treatment rate of domestic sewage in downtown Beijing will reach 98% by the end of 2015. 7. Recycled water in Beijing is mainly used for industry, landscaping, and cleaning. 8. Many new communities in southern Beijing have a network to recycle water. 9. In the past, people were not enthusiastic about using recycled water, but as the quality has improved and more plants have been set up, more residents are gradually accepting it. 10. The government will replace traditional coal-burning stoves with electric radiators as part of a three-year plan. 11. Since 2010, southern Beijing has been developed from an area with low-end industries to a commercial center and educational hub. Now, I'll look at each of the answer choices and see which one is supported by the passage. Choice A: "Recycled water will be mainly used for drink." - The passage states that the capital's recycled water is mainly used for industry, landscaping, and cleaning. There's no mention of it being used mainly for drinking. So this choice is false. Choice B: "As usual, recycled water is accepted by people." - The passage says that in the past, people were not enthusiastic about using recycled water, but now as the quality has improved and more plants have been set up, more residents are gradually accepting it. So it's not as usual that people accept it. It has changed from past behavior. Therefore, this choice is false. Choice C: "Traditionally people in southern Beijing use coal-burning stove for cooking." - The passage mentions that the government will replace traditional coal-burning stoves with electric radiators as part of a three-year plan.
